meta.title: Kubernetes Error Reference
meta.description: Structured reference of Kubernetes pod states, node conditions, deployment errors, and fixes.

errors.name: Container Crash Loop
errors.cause: The container starts, crashes, is restarted by Kubernetes, crashes again, and so on. Kubernetes applies exponential backoff between restarts (10s, 20s, 40s... up to 5 minutes).
errors.fix: Check logs from the last crash: `kubectl logs <pod> --previous`. Describe the pod: `kubectl describe pod <pod>`. Common causes: application startup failure, missing env vars, missing config, bad entrypoint command.

errors.name: Image Pull Back Off
errors.cause: Kubernetes cannot pull the container image. Kubernetes retries with exponential backoff after repeated failures.
errors.fix: Verify the image name and tag. Check registry credentials: `kubectl get secret <secret>`. Create an imagePullSecret if using a private registry. Check registry availability from the node.

errors.name: Image Pull Error
errors.cause: Initial failure to pull the image before backoff kicks in. Image name typo, tag not found, or auth failure.
errors.fix: Run `kubectl describe pod <pod>` for the exact error. Test the pull manually on the node. Verify registry credentials and imagePullPolicy.

errors.name: Out of Memory Killed
errors.cause: The container exceeded its memory limit and was killed by the Linux OOM killer. The container's `resources.limits.memory` was hit.
errors.fix: Increase memory limits in the pod spec. Profile the application for memory leaks. Set realistic `requests` and `limits`. Check `kubectl describe pod <pod>` for `OOMKilled: true` and `Reason: OOMKilled`.

errors.name: Pod Evicted
errors.cause: The node ran out of resources (disk, memory, PID) and Kubernetes evicted pods to reclaim resources. Pods without requests/limits or those below the QoS threshold are evicted first.
errors.fix: Set `resources.requests` and `resources.limits` on all containers. Increase node capacity. Check node disk pressure: `kubectl describe node <node>`. Clean up old logs and unused images on nodes.

errors.name: Pod Stuck in Pending
errors.cause: Kubernetes cannot schedule the pod. Common causes: insufficient resources on any node, node selectors or affinity rules cannot be satisfied, taints without tolerations, PVC not bound.
errors.fix: Run `kubectl describe pod <pod>` and look at Events. Check node capacity: `kubectl describe nodes`. Verify node selectors, affinity, and taints match available nodes.

errors.name: Container Config Error
errors.cause: Kubernetes cannot create the container due to a misconfiguration. Usually a missing Secret, ConfigMap, or invalid volume mount.
errors.fix: Run `kubectl describe pod <pod>`. Check that referenced Secrets and ConfigMaps exist in the same namespace: `kubectl get secret,configmap -n <namespace>`.

errors.name: Run Container Error
errors.cause: Container started but failed immediately, often due to a bad command, missing binary, or wrong working directory.
errors.fix: Check `kubectl describe pod <pod>` for the specific error. Verify the container command and entrypoint in the pod spec against what the image provides.

errors.name: Invalid Image Name
errors.cause: The image name in the pod spec is malformed (e.g. contains uppercase, invalid characters, or a bad registry URL format).
errors.fix: Correct the image name in the deployment/pod spec. Image names must be lowercase. Validate: `docker pull <image>` locally.

errors.name: PostStart Lifecycle Hook Error
errors.cause: The postStart lifecycle hook command failed. Kubernetes blocks the container from reaching Running state until the hook completes.
errors.fix: Debug the hook command in `kubectl describe pod`. Run the command manually in a test container. Ensure the hook is idempotent and handles race conditions with container startup.

errors.name: PreStop Hook Timeout
errors.cause: The preStop hook ran for longer than `terminationGracePeriodSeconds` and was killed before completing.
errors.fix: Increase `terminationGracePeriodSeconds`. Optimize the preStop hook to complete quickly. Ensure graceful shutdown is handled in the application itself, using preStop only for coordination.

errors.name: Node Not Ready
errors.cause: The node is unhealthy and not accepting workloads. Causes include kubelet crash, network partition, disk pressure, memory pressure, or PID pressure.
errors.fix: SSH to the node and check kubelet: `systemctl status kubelet`. Check node conditions: `kubectl describe node <node>`. Drain and cordon the node if needed: `kubectl drain <node> --ignore-daemonsets`.

errors.name: Node Disk Pressure
errors.cause: Available disk space on the node is below the threshold (default: 10% or 100Mi free). Kubernetes will start evicting pods.
errors.fix: SSH to the node and free disk space. Remove unused container images: `crictl rmi --prune`. Clean up old logs. Increase node disk size. Set `evictionHard` thresholds in kubelet config.

errors.name: Node Memory Pressure
errors.cause: Available memory on the node is below the eviction threshold. Kubernetes will start evicting BestEffort and Burstable pods.
errors.fix: Check memory usage on the node. Review pod resource requests and limits. Move pods with high memory usage to larger nodes. Add more nodes to the cluster.

errors.name: Node PID Pressure
errors.cause: The number of processes on the node is approaching the system PID limit. Can be caused by fork bombs, runaway processes, or too many containers.
errors.fix: Identify runaway processes: `ps aux --sort=-%cpu | head`. Set PID limits per pod: `resources.limits.ephemeral-storage`. Increase system PID limits: `kernel.pid_max` sysctl.

errors.name: Node Network Not Ready
errors.cause: The CNI (Container Network Interface) plugin is not running or failed. Pods cannot get IP addresses.
errors.fix: Check CNI pod logs: `kubectl logs -n kube-system <cni-pod>`. Restart the CNI daemonset. Verify CNI binary is installed on the node at `/opt/cni/bin/`.

errors.name: Deployment Progress Deadline Exceeded
errors.cause: The deployment did not make progress within `progressDeadlineSeconds` (default: 600s). New pods are not becoming Ready.
errors.fix: Check pod status: `kubectl get pods`. Describe the deployment: `kubectl describe deployment <name>`. Look for failed pod events. Check image, resources, and readiness probes.

errors.name: ReplicaSet Failed to Create Pods
errors.cause: The ReplicaSet controller cannot create pods. Common causes: resource quota exceeded, RBAC missing, invalid pod spec.
errors.fix: Check ReplicaSet events: `kubectl describe rs <name>`. Check namespace resource quotas: `kubectl describe resourcequota -n <namespace>`.

errors.name: HPA Cannot Scale
errors.cause: HPA cannot scale because: metrics server is not installed, the deployment is missing resource requests (required for CPU-based scaling), or max replicas is already reached.
errors.fix: Install metrics-server. Set `resources.requests.cpu` on the container. Check HPA status: `kubectl describe hpa <name>`. Increase `maxReplicas` if needed.

errors.name: Service Has No Endpoints
errors.cause: The Service selector does not match any running pod labels, or all matched pods are not Ready.
errors.fix: Check endpoints: `kubectl get endpoints <service>`. Verify pod labels match the service selector: `kubectl get pods --show-labels`. Check pod readiness probes.

errors.name: LoadBalancer External IP Pending
errors.cause: A Service of type LoadBalancer has been created but no external IP is assigned. The cloud provider controller has not provisioned a load balancer.
errors.fix: Verify the cloud controller manager is running. Check service events: `kubectl describe service <name>`. On local clusters (kind, minikube) use NodePort or a tool like MetalLB.

errors.name: Ingress Backend Service Not Found
errors.cause: The Ingress resource references a backend Service that does not exist or has the wrong port.
errors.fix: Verify the service name and port in the Ingress spec match an existing Service: `kubectl get svc -n <namespace>`. Check ingress controller logs.

errors.name: CoreDNS Resolution Failure
errors.cause: Pods cannot resolve DNS names. CoreDNS may be crashed, misconfigured, or a network policy is blocking DNS traffic.
errors.fix: Check CoreDNS pods: `kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l k8s-app=kube-dns`. Test DNS from a pod: `kubectl exec <pod> -- nslookup kubernetes.default`. Check NetworkPolicy for port 53 blocking.

errors.name: NetworkPolicy Blocking Traffic
errors.cause: A NetworkPolicy is blocking traffic between pods or from pods to external services. Once any NetworkPolicy selects a pod, all traffic not explicitly allowed is denied.
errors.fix: Review NetworkPolicy rules: `kubectl get networkpolicy -n <namespace>`. Test with a temporary allow-all policy. Use `kubectl describe networkpolicy` to see selectors and rules.

errors.name: PersistentVolumeClaim Stuck Pending
errors.cause: No PersistentVolume matches the PVC (access mode, storage class, size). Or the StorageClass provisioner is not running.
errors.fix: Check PVC status: `kubectl describe pvc <name>`. List available PVs: `kubectl get pv`. Verify StorageClass exists and provisioner is running: `kubectl get storageclass`.

errors.name: PersistentVolumeClaim Lost
errors.cause: The underlying PersistentVolume was deleted or became unavailable while the claim was still in use. Often caused by manually deleting PVs.
errors.fix: This may result in data loss. Restore the volume from backup if available. Never manually delete PVs that are bound to PVCs. Use `reclaimPolicy: Retain` on StorageClasses for critical data.

errors.name: Secret Not Found
errors.cause: A pod, deployment, or ingress references a Secret that does not exist in the same namespace.
errors.fix: Create the missing secret: `kubectl create secret generic <name> --from-literal=key=value -n <namespace>`. Verify namespace: `kubectl get secrets -n <namespace>`.

errors.name: ConfigMap Not Found
errors.cause: A pod spec references a ConfigMap that does not exist in the same namespace.
errors.fix: Create the ConfigMap: `kubectl create configmap <name> --from-file=config.yaml -n <namespace>`. Use `kubectl get configmap -n <namespace>` to verify.

errors.name: RBAC Permission Denied
errors.cause: A pod or user is attempting an API action they do not have permission for. The ServiceAccount lacks the required Role or ClusterRole binding.
errors.fix: Check RBAC: `kubectl auth can-i <verb> <resource> --as=system:serviceaccount:<namespace>:<sa>`. Create a Role and RoleBinding granting the required permissions.

errors.name: ServiceAccount Not Found
errors.cause: The pod spec references a ServiceAccount that does not exist in the namespace.
errors.fix: Create the ServiceAccount: `kubectl create serviceaccount <name> -n <namespace>`. Or set `serviceAccountName: default` to use the default account.

errors.name: Pod Security Admission Violation
errors.cause: The pod spec violates the Pod Security Standards policy enforced on the namespace (e.g. requesting privileged mode, hostNetwork, or running as root in a restricted namespace).
errors.fix: Check namespace labels: `kubectl get namespace <ns> --show-labels`. Adjust pod spec to comply with the policy (remove privileged, set runAsNonRoot, set seccompProfile). Or relax the namespace policy.

errors.name: Readiness Probe Failing
errors.cause: The readiness probe is failing, so the pod is excluded from Service endpoints. The container is running but not passing health checks.
errors.fix: Check probe configuration in `kubectl describe pod`. Test the probe endpoint manually from inside the pod: `kubectl exec <pod> -- curl localhost:<port>/health`. Increase `initialDelaySeconds` if the app takes time to start.

errors.name: Liveness Probe Failing
errors.cause: The liveness probe is failing, causing Kubernetes to restart the container. The application may have deadlocked or stopped responding.
errors.fix: Inspect pod events: `kubectl describe pod`. Check if the probe threshold is too aggressive. Test the probe endpoint: `kubectl exec <pod> -- curl localhost:<port>/live`. Increase `failureThreshold` or `timeoutSeconds`.

errors.name: Startup Probe Failing
errors.cause: The startup probe did not succeed within `failureThreshold * periodSeconds`. Kubernetes kills the container before it finishes initializing.
errors.fix: Increase `failureThreshold` or `periodSeconds` on the startup probe to give the app more time. Check application startup logs: `kubectl logs <pod>`.

errors.name: Resource Quota Exceeded
errors.cause: Creating or updating resources in the namespace would exceed the configured ResourceQuota (CPU, memory, pod count, service count, etc.).
errors.fix: Check quota usage: `kubectl describe resourcequota -n <namespace>`. Delete unused resources or increase quota. Ensure all pods have resource requests set (required for quota enforcement).

errors.name: LimitRange Violation
errors.cause: A pod or container is requesting resources outside the range defined by a LimitRange in the namespace.
errors.fix: Check LimitRange: `kubectl describe limitrange -n <namespace>`. Adjust container resource requests and limits to fall within the defined min/max values.

errors.name: StatefulSet Rolling Update Stuck
errors.cause: A StatefulSet rolling update is stuck because a pod is not becoming Ready (often due to PVC issues, readiness probe failures, or anti-affinity blocking scheduling).
errors.fix: Check the stuck pod: `kubectl describe pod <statefulset>-<ordinal>`. StatefulSets update in reverse ordinal order and will not proceed past a stuck pod. Fix the underlying pod issue.

errors.name: Job Backoff Limit Exceeded
errors.cause: All Job pods failed and the retry limit (`backoffLimit`) has been reached. Kubernetes stops retrying.
errors.fix: Check failed pod logs: `kubectl logs <job-pod>`. Fix the underlying issue and recreate the Job. Increase `backoffLimit` if transient failures are expected.

errors.name: CronJob Missed Schedule
errors.cause: The CronJob missed its scheduled execution window. This can happen if the controller was down or if a previous job run took longer than the scheduled interval.
errors.fix: Check `startingDeadlineSeconds` on the CronJob. Set `concurrencyPolicy: Forbid` to prevent overlapping runs. Verify the CronJob schedule with a cron expression validator.

errors.name: Ingress TLS Certificate Secret Not Found
errors.cause: The Ingress TLS section references a Secret that does not exist or has not yet been provisioned by cert-manager.
errors.fix: Check cert-manager Certificate resources: `kubectl get certificate -n <namespace>`. Describe the Certificate to see provisioning errors. Manually create the Secret if cert-manager is not being used.

errors.name: etcd Quorum Lost
errors.cause: More than half of etcd members are unavailable, causing the cluster to become read-only. The API server cannot write state.
errors.fix: This is a critical control plane failure. Restore etcd from a snapshot. For a 3-node cluster, losing 2 nodes loses quorum. Always run etcd with an odd number of members (3 or 5).

errors.name: API Server Unreachable
errors.cause: The Kubernetes API server is down or unreachable. kubectl commands fail with connection refused or timeout errors.
errors.fix: Check API server pod: `kubectl get pods -n kube-system` (if possible). SSH to control plane node: `docker ps | grep apiserver` or `crictl ps | grep apiserver`. Check logs and restart if needed.

errors.name: kube-scheduler Unhealthy
errors.cause: The scheduler pod is crashed or leader election failed. New pods will stay in Pending state indefinitely.
errors.fix: Check scheduler pod: `kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-scheduler`. Restart the scheduler pod. Check leader election: `kubectl get lease -n kube-system`.

errors.name: kube-controller-manager Unhealthy
errors.cause: The controller manager is not running. Deployments, ReplicaSets, DaemonSets, and Jobs will not reconcile.
errors.fix: Check controller-manager pod: `kubectl get pods -n kube-system -l component=kube-controller-manager`. Restart the pod. Check logs for certificate or RBAC issues.
